verdict_reasoning: FN1 encodes fibronectin, a large secreted glycoprotein. While soluble plasma fibronectin circulates, cellular fibronectin is assembled into a fibrillar extracellular matrix via integrin-mediated (α5β1) binding and self-polymerization on the cell surface — this matrix assembly is wash-resistant and FN fibrils are tightly cell-associated. Additionally, oncofetal fibronectin isoforms (EDA/EDB containing) are surface/matrix-accessible tumor targets with active ADC/radioligand programs (e.g., L19-based therapeutics targeting EDB). This constitutes stable post-translational attachment to the cell surface via integrin engagement and fibril assembly, beyond transient recruitment.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Borderline between secreted_only (matrix deposition) and stable_surface_attachment; fibril assembly on integrins is wash-resistant but ECM-localized rather than PM-anchored per se.
